'''Gareth Haggerty''' (born 8 September 1981) is a former [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer. He played for the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[Co-operative Championship]]. He previously played for [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]], [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] and the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] all of whom play in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk/widnes-vikings/widnes-vikings-news/2009/10/06/widnes-vikings-irish-international-prop-gareth-haggerty-returns-with-super-league-experience-while-teenage-wigan-back-row-forward-chris-gerrard-signs-in-55368-24865416|title=Runcorn and Widnes - News, views, gossip, pictures, video - Liverpool Echo|website=www.runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk}}</ref>

Haggerty's usual position was as a {{rlp|pr}}. He is a specialist impact player, coming off the interchange bench.

==Background==
Haggerty was born in [[St Helens, Merseyside]], England.

He is the son of former [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] forward [[Roy Haggerty]].

==Career==
He was a member of the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Ireland|Ireland]] squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Cassidy included in Ireland squad">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7657596.stm|title=Cassidy included in Ireland squad|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-07|access-date=2008-10-07}}</ref> Although relatively unknown in Australia prior to the tournament, his performances from the bench in the World Cup earned widespread praise from both fans and pundits, with many Australians going as far as to chant 'sign him up' during games.{{Citation needed|date=November 2022}}
